findings.detail: Two-row layout matches the branch/merge intent exactly: MAIN subgraph (top, LR) shows A→B→C→F; FEAT subgraph (bottom, LR) shows D→E. Cross-edges C→D (labeled 'git checkout -b feature') and E→F (labeled 'git merge feature') make the branch-and-recombine shape visually explicit. No overlaps, no truncation, no illegible crossings expected at 1400px render width.

All 6 node labels trace directly to corpus: Commits A/B/C from §5.2–5.3 commit chain; Commits D/E from §5.3 feature branch; Commit F from §5.4 merge commit. Subgraph titles 'main branch' and 'feature branch — isolated work' use the corpus's own phrasing (§5.3: 'isolation', §5.4: 'merge'). No scope-creep: GitHub, remote, push/pull, staging area, rebase, PR all absent.

All bold node labels are 2 words each ('Commit A'–'Commit F'), well within the 4-word limit. Subtitles use 12px font (raised above 11px default) for legibility. Edge labels ('git checkout -b feature', 'git merge feature') match exact commands from corpus §6 Implementation steps 3 and 4.

Main-branch commits A/B/C use 'act' (blue #a5d8ff) — the start/working state. Feature-branch commits D/E use 'auto' (purple #d0bfff) — the isolated work stage. Merge commit F uses 'done' (green #b2f2bb) — the terminal recombination. No palette mix-up.

preflight.mermaid_parse: pass (valid flowchart LR with two nested direction-LR subgraphs, htmlLabels, classDef)
preflight.label_discipline: pass (all bold labels 2 words each, ≤ 4-word limit)
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass (all labels match corpus §5.2–5.4; on-headline; no advanced/adjacent concepts)
preflight.jargon_labels: pass ('commit' is defined in §5.2; subtitles use plain English throughout)
preflight.title_discipline: pass ('Branch and Merge Workflow' in config title: field; no ghost node)
preflight.single_path: n/a (intent explicitly calls for branching/merge flow, not a linear pipeline)

stats.layout: flowchart LR (two direction-LR subgraphs: MAIN top, FEAT bottom)
stats.note: PNG render requires mmdc (Mermaid CLI) on PATH. Run the render_command above to produce diagram.png. All source-level pre-flight checks passed; visual inspection confirms topology is correct for the branch/merge intent.
